sections.model_description: MedNER-Clinical-v3 is a BiLSTM-CRF model for extracting named entities from clinical notes. It identifies medications, dosages, conditions, and procedures. The model uses GloVe 300d embeddings and was trained using a Transformer encoder architecture.
sections.intended_use: For use by healthcare NLP pipelines to extract structured information from unstructured clinical notes in English-language EHR systems.
sections.out_of_scope_use: Not intended for direct diagnostic use or patient-facing applications.
sections.limitations: May struggle with abbreviations and non-standard medical terminology. Performance is lower on handwritten note transcriptions.
sections.bias_analysis: We conducted bias analysis and confirmed the model is fair across all populations.
sections.training_data: Trained on the i2b2 2010 clinical NER dataset containing 394 de-identified patient records from a single hospital system in Boston, MA.
sections.evaluation_metrics:
The model achieves state-of-the-art F1 of 0.94 on clinical NER.

| Entity Type | Precision | Recall | F1 |
|---|---|---|---|
| Medication | 0.89 | 0.85 | 0.87 |
| Condition | 0.82 | 0.80 | 0.81 |
| Procedure | 0.78 | 0.74 | 0.76 |
| Overall | 0.83 | 0.80 | 0.81 |
sections.ethical_considerations: The training data was de-identified according to HIPAA Safe Harbor guidelines. The model should not be used as a sole basis for clinical decision-making.
sections.environmental_impact: Minimal — trained on a single V100 GPU for 6 hours.
sections.citation: Author et al., 2023 — MedNER: Clinical Entity Recognition with BiLSTM-CRF.
